name: Jan Savitt
description: Russian-born American bandleader, musical arranger, and violinist
abstract: Jan Savitt (born Jacob Sarvetnick; September 4, 1907 – October 4, 1948), known as "The Stokowski of Swing", from having played violin in Leopold Stokowski's orchestra, was an American bandleader, musical arranger, and violinist.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I thought it was Jan Savitt or something like that. [INTERVIEWER] 'Bon Bon' was with Jan Savitt.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: I arranged for Jan Savitt... I came back, oh, I went to work with Savitt... I worked with him and wrote a couple of arrangements
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: Jan Savitt played that room. ... She used all the “hot” bands at that hotel, the Edison Hotel, and the Lincoln Hotel he used all the “hot” bands like Artie Shaw and Charlie Barnet and Jan Savitt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...scored regularly for Ida Ray Hutton, Glenn Miller, Jan Savitt, Artie Shaw. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, that's what it was.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: But most of the guys had spent years playing with Benny Goodman or Woody Herman or Buddy Rich or Jan Savitt and his Top Hatters...
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: KYW, which was NBC had Jan Savitt and the Top Hatters... So I used to hear them all the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
